play.description: Known for his two-way impact and reliable shooting stroke, OG Anunoby has established himself as one of the league's most versatile wings. With the Knicks building momentum early in Game 1 of the Eastern Conference semifinals against Philadelphia, the forward rejects the opposition, gets the ball in transition and attacks the basket with purpose. He navigates through defenders and finishes a crafty driving layup that exemplifies his basketball IQ and scoring touch. Anunoby's efficient 18-point performance on 87.5% shooting helped fuel New York's dominant 39-point victory.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: In a battle between Eastern Conference contenders, every defensive possession carries weight. OG Anunoby showcased his elite two-way ability with a perfectly timed block that helped shift momentum in New York's favor. The versatile forward scored all 18 of his points in the second half to complement these defensive gems, providing crucial contributions as the Knicks edged Miami 132-125 behind Jalen Brunson's season-high 47 points at Madison Square Garden.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: OG Anunoby's best defensive plays don't always end on that end of the floor. Against Minnesota on November 5, 2025, the long-armed forward leaps to tip a Timberwolves pass, corrals the steal and immediately pushes it ahead to Jalen Brunson in transition. Anunoby never stops running. Brunson finds him streaking to the rim and lobs it up for a soaring two-handed slam that shook Madison Square Garden. Anunoby finished with 25 points, eight rebounds and two steals in New York's 137-114 rout.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: OG Anunoby's defensive reputation precedes him, but the versatile forward proved his offensive arsenal runs deep in the Knicks' season opener. With New York leading late in the fourth quarter against Cleveland at Madison Square Garden, Anunoby received a pinpoint outlet pass from Jalen Brunson in transition. The 6'7" forward broke free with no defenders in sight, elevated at the rim and threw down a spectacular running reverse dunk that sent the home crowd into a frenzy.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: May 25, 2025 had the inside track towards becoming one of Indianapolis sports’ most eventful weekends, given a trio that included the Eastern Conference Finals, a high-profile women’s basketball clash, and the 109th annual Indy 500. As confirmed through OG Anunoby and the New York Knicks, though, the path certainly wouldn’t come without a roadblock or two. Amped up for a critical late-game possession with New York sporting a once-unlikely four-point lead, Anunoby metamorphoses into a brick wall, barricading Aaron Nesmith’s path to the rim forcefully. Similar to that of an older brother battling a younger sibling in the family driveway, Anunoby closes with a good ‘ol fashioned “get that out of here,” positioning New York for a fruitful finish just four minutes later. 0-9 in Gainbridge Fieldhouse upon Game Three’s arrival, the Knicks displayed champion-like resilience, erasing a 20-point deficit during the season-saving win. Anunoby’s contribution arrived through 16 points (on just nine shots), and a two-rebound, two-assist, one-steal, three-block, four-triple complement.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: When the already-starpowered New York Knicks signed OG Anunoby to their franchise’s largest guaranteed contract last summer, expectations were that a specific type of excellence would then follow when high-pressure postseason games came about. Against the defending-champion Boston Celtics in Game One of 2025’s East Semifinals, Anunoby devoted a 46-minute masterpiece to checking every potential box possible. Seemingly moving at a different speed than Boston throughout a come-from-behind second half, Anunoby uses said pace advantageously, jetting past Jayson Tatum with his across-the-court teammate already clued in on the could-be highlight. Given little room for liftoff upon catching the basketball promptly at the rim, Anunoby handles the job anyways, powering into a two-handed and-one to all but steal momentum in overtime. Turning a 20-point deficit into yesterday’s news, Anunoby steered a Game One comeback through two-way brilliance. Alongside holding opposing Celtics to 1-of-15 (6.6 percent) shooting as the primary defender, Anunoby complemented it through 29 points (on 10-of-20 shooting), four rebounds, three assists, one steal, and six 3-pointers on May 5, 2025.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: Likely aware of the many questions surrounding the New York Knicks’ championship potential and ability to beat the NBA’s top dogs, OG Anunoby arrived to February 28, 2025’s clash bearing a message of own. Before he spoke though, one unskippable step was required first: silencing the Memphis Grizzlies’ fired-up home crowd. Accustomed to high-stakes situations after experiencing a title push in 2019, and other reasonably-deep runs thereafter, the New York Knicks’ two-way swingman lifts up the perimeter on the weak side, knowing the attention that his All-NBA teammate’s isolation would command. Calm and confident in his raise, Anunoby connects on a cold-blooded trifecta, affording New York a one-point lead in the final seconds. Sorely needing a win over one of the league’s premier teams, the Knicks found success here, as they moved to 39-20. Joining five others in the double-figure scoring column, Anunoby concluded the night with 19 points (on 7-of-17 shooting), three rebounds, three assists, one steal, two blocks, and four treys.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: From the franchise record-tying 45 assists to the 19 3-point makes, the New York Knicks could’ve scanned almost any part of the scoreboard to confirm just how offensively connected they were during November 25, 2024’s 27-point win. If OG Anunoby had any say in the matter, though, the full story wouldn’t be accurately told until such excellence also applied to the floor’s less glamorous side. No. 1 in contested shots among all perimeter talents before meeting the Denver Nuggets, Anunoby conducts a help defense clinic, helping his All-Star teammate offset Nikola Jokić’s inside-the-arc repertoire with a well-timed block. Unfinished, the do-it-all swingman summons the energy to conquer a loose ball battle and then a one-on-one fastbreak clash against Christian Braun, barreling through the middle for a soft-touch scoop finish. The sequence added another footnote to Anunoby’s noticeably-heightened approach against Denver, who’ve absorbed Anunoby’s highest scoring average (17.3) outside of his former team. In a déjà vu-like manner from a season ago, Anunoby’s eruption encouraged a career-best 40 points (on 16-of-23 shooting), five rebounds, four assists, one block, five 3-pointers, and a +21 plus-minus.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: Upon learning about how unfriendly the 3-point arc would be to their attempts on December 1, 2024, the New Orleans Pelicans’ approach of attacking the rim instead, theoretically at least, made a ton of sense. That is, until OG Anunoby arrived on a defensive pick-and-roll tag, prepared to freeze New Orleans’ plans with additional bad news in the paint. Successful in helping silence the opposition’s attack on nearly every possession already, Anunoby rotates over, positioning himself for another will-imposing sequence, immediately after recognizing Daniel Theis’ intent at a drive-and-score. Through sound timing, the 2022-23 All-Defensive Teamer elevates, denying entry through a physical rejection that leaves both players floorbound. New York ultimately kept the Pelicans grounded, using postseason-like intensity to hold the evening’s opponents to rough finishes from deep (14.8 percent on 27 attempts) and interior (46.3 percent on 67 attempts), alongside 19 combined steals and blocks. Versatility was Anunoby’s calling card, as he encouraged victory through a seven-point, four-rebound, two-assist, three-steal, four-block blend.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: Busy at work, using a 22-point avalanche to keep his New York Knicks within striking distance of the Indiana Pacers in the first half, OG Anunoby made certain that his final contributions would serve as the knockout blow. Maneuvering through Game Two with the look of a player boasting a championship background and big-game pedigree, Anunoby finds himself stationed along the far right corner with the ball swinging his way, and a chance to finally wrestle away the Pacers’ lead. Eye-to-eye with a player he hoisted a championship with just five years prior, Anunoby loads up, launching a 3-pointer over Pascal Siakam’s contest, fittingly providing the Knicks with a three-point advantage they would never again relinquish.  A hamstring injury did end his night after just three quarters, but not before Anunoby was able to etch his signature into the history books with a dynamic performance, propelling New York to a 2-0 series open. Needing just 28 minutes to put his imprint on the marquee win, Anunoby worked in perfect cohesion with two other 20-point scorers, recording 28 points, four rebounds, three assists, one rejection, and four 3-pointers on May 8, 2024.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: Formidable in their own right, the New York Knicks have proven to be an entirely different animal with OG Anunoby in the rotation. Appropriately bringing the defense against the defending-champion Denver Nuggets, the talented swingman hounds a passing lane with ambition, intercepting a pass to set up a free runway in transition. Undaunted by the trailing defender breathing down his neck, Anunoby shields the ball perfectly, fighting through contact on the way to a graceful and-one finish. Continuing his trend of sustained excellence against the Nuggets, Anunoby helped deal Denver their largest loss of the season to date on January 25, 2024, leading the way with a team-high 26 points, six steals, and three 3-pointers in a signature home win.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: The OG has landed in the Big Apple. Suiting up in orange and blue for the first time, OG Anunoby earned instant fanfare and applause from the Madison Square Garden crowd in this matinee from January 1, 2024. Finding a complementary rhythm alonside Donte Divincenzo, Anunoby made cutting a habit in his first game and was rewarded with a series of easy finishes around the rim. Anunoby finished the game with 17 points as the Knicks won their first game of 2024 in an impressive win over the Minnesota Timberwolves.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: Calm, cool and collected at all stages of a ball game, OG Anunoby knows exactly where to be and what to do when he gets there. Tracking an offensive rebound as it comes off the rim in the closing seconds of a two-point ball game, Anunoby scoots into position to secure the rock and buries a floater to force overtime. Anunoby finished the night with 24 points and a pair of steals while his Toronto Raptors held on to secure the November 5, 2023 victory.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: Play busy, stay busy. O.G. Anunoby has made a career doing just that. Left alone in the corner as his Toronto Raptors teammates create a shot attempt, Anunoby flies under the radar of his defender, leaps along the baseline and throws down an uncontested putback slam. Anunoby’s relentless efforts on both ends of the floor were evident as early as Year 1 and he finished the January 26, 2018 contest with five points and a trio of steals.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: O.G. Anunoby’s quest to lead the NBA in steals got off to a fast start. The Toronto Raptors forward was relentless on the defensive end as he came up with a swipe of an overmatched De’Andre Hunter before again getting the best of Hunter by finishing the sequence with a transition layup. Anunoby posted 14 points, six rebounds, three assists, six steals and two blocks in Toronto’s 139-109 win over the Atlanta Hawks on October 31, 2022. The six thefts were tied for second-most in a game during the 2022-23 season, and Anunoby led the league with 128 steals and an average of 1.9 per game.
play.headline: OG Anunoby

play.description: During a night he matched his season-high in points, O.G. Anunoby still found time to be an unselfish teammate. When Anunoby caught a pass in the post and was immediately ensnared in an Orlando Magic double team, some quick thinking allowed him to drop off a beauty of a no-look pass to an open Christian Koloko for the two-handed flush. Anunoby poured in a game-high 32 points on 12 of 17 shooting and added four assists and three steals as he led the Toronto Raptors to a 121-108 win on December 3, 2022.
play.headline: OG Anunoby
